modules.title: Introduction to C Programming
topics: History and significance of the C language
Setting up the development environment (GCC, IDE)
Understanding the C compilation and linking process
Basic structure of a C program (preprocessor, main, body)
handsOn: Install GCC and configure VS Code for C development
Compile and run your first "Hello, World!" program
modules.learningOutcome: Understand the history, compilation workflow, and fundamental structure of C programs.

modules.title: Data Types, Variables & Operators
topics: Primitive data types: int, float, double, char, void
Variable declaration, initialization, and naming rules
Constants: const keyword and #define
Arithmetic operators (+, -, *, /, %)
handsOn: Write programs demonstrating each data type and conversion
Build a simple calculator using arithmetic operators
modules.learningOutcome: Declare and manipulate data correctly using C's type system and operator set.

modules.title: Control Flow Statements
topics: if, if-else, and nested if-else
switch-case statements
for loop
while loop
handsOn: Build number-guessing and menu-driven programs
Pattern-printing programs (triangles, pyramids, diamonds)
modules.learningOutcome: Control program execution flow using C's conditional statements and loop constructs.

modules.title: Functions in C
topics: Function declaration, definition, and calling convention
Return types and parameter passing
Recursion and recursive problem-solving techniques
Pass by value mechanics
handsOn: Implement recursive functions: factorial, Fibonacci, power
Build modular calculator programs using multiple functions
modules.learningOutcome: Design modular and reusable programs using C functions and understand variable scope and lifetime.

modules.title: Arrays & Strings
topics: One-dimensional arrays: declaration, initialization, traversal
Multi-dimensional arrays (2D matrices)
Character arrays and C-style strings
strlen, strcpy, strcat, strcmp
handsOn: Matrix addition and multiplication programs
String manipulation: reverse, palindrome checker, word counter
modules.learningOutcome: Store and manipulate sequential and textual data using C arrays and character strings.

modules.title: Pointers in C
topics: Pointer concept: memory addresses and the address-of operator (&)
Pointer declaration, initialization, and dereferencing (*)
Pointer arithmetic and array traversal via pointers
Pointers and functions: pass by reference
handsOn: Swap function using pass by reference with pointers
Array traversal and manipulation using pointer arithmetic
modules.learningOutcome: Understand and apply pointers for direct memory access, reference semantics, and advanced program design.

modules.title: Structures & Unions
topics: Structure definition, declaration, and initialization
Accessing structure members using dot (.) and arrow (->) operators
Nested structures and arrays of structures
Pointers to structures
handsOn: Student record management system using arrays of structures
Demonstrate union memory sharing with type punning
modules.learningOutcome: Group and manage related data efficiently using structures and unions in C.

modules.title: File I/O in C
topics: File handling concepts and the FILE type
Opening and closing files: fopen(), fclose()
Text file operations: fprintf(), fscanf(), fgets(), fputs()
Binary file operations: fread(), fwrite()
handsOn: Read and write student records to a text file
Binary file copy program
modules.learningOutcome: Implement persistent data storage and retrieval using C file I/O operations.

modules.title: Dynamic Memory Management
topics: Stack vs heap memory: differences and use cases
Dynamic allocation functions: malloc(), calloc(), realloc()
Freeing memory: free() and avoiding double-free errors
Memory leaks: causes, detection, and prevention
handsOn: Dynamically allocated array with runtime resizing
Implement a singly linked list using malloc and free
modules.learningOutcome: Manage program memory dynamically for flexible and efficient data structure design.

modules.title: Preprocessor Directives & Header Files
topics: Macro constants and function-like macros with #define
File inclusion: #include for standard and custom headers
Conditional compilation: #ifdef, #ifndef, #if, #endif
#pragma directives (once, pack)
handsOn: Create a custom header file and link multiple source files
Use conditional compilation for debug vs release builds
modules.learningOutcome: Organize large codebases using the C preprocessor, header files, and multi-file compilation.

modules.title: Standard C Library
topics: stdio.h: formatted I/O functions
stdlib.h: utility functions — atoi(), atof(), qsort(), rand(), exit()
string.h: complete string manipulation reference
math.h: mathematical functions — pow(), sqrt(), floor(), ceil()
modules.learningOutcome: Leverage the C Standard Library to avoid reinventing common utilities and write efficient programs.

modules.title: Error Handling & Debugging
topics: errno variable and perror() for system error messages
assert() macro for defensive programming
Introduction to GDB debugger: breakpoints, stepping, watchpoints
Detecting memory errors with Valgrind (memcheck)
modules.learningOutcome: Identify, handle, and debug errors in C programs using industry-standard tools.

modules.title: Mini Project: Console Application
topics: Project planning: requirements, design, and data modeling
Applying: arrays, structures, pointers, file I/O, and functions
Writing clean, documented, and modular C code
handsOn: Build a Library Management or Student Record Management System
Implement search, add, update, delete, and file persistence features
modules.learningOutcome: Integrate all core C concepts into a complete, functional, and well-structured console application.

modules.title: Data Structures in C
topics: Singly and doubly linked lists: insertion, deletion, traversal
Circular linked lists
Stack and queue implementation using arrays and linked lists
Binary trees: creation, traversals (inorder, preorder, postorder)
handsOn: Implement a linked list with full insert, delete, search operations
Build a stack-based infix-to-postfix expression converter
modules.learningOutcome: Implement fundamental data structures from scratch in C for efficient data management.

modules.title: Algorithms & Complexity Analysis
topics: Time complexity: Big-O, Big-Ω, Big-Θ notation
Space complexity analysis
Sorting algorithms: bubble, selection, insertion, merge, quicksort
Searching: linear search and binary search
handsOn: Implement and benchmark merge sort vs quicksort
Recursive backtracking: N-Queens and Sudoku solver
modules.learningOutcome: Analyze algorithm efficiency and implement classic sorting and searching algorithms in C.

modules.title: System Programming in C
topics: POSIX API overview and standards
Process management: fork(), exec(), wait(), getpid()
Pipes (pipe(), read(), write())
Shared memory (shmget, shmat, shmdt)
handsOn: Fork-based parent-child process communication program
Pipe communication between two processes
modules.learningOutcome: Write system-level programs using POSIX APIs for process management and inter-process communication.

modules.title: Multithreading with POSIX Threads
topics: Thread creation and termination: pthread_create(), pthread_join()
Thread synchronization: mutex locks (pthread_mutex_t)
Semaphores for resource control (sem_init, sem_wait, sem_post)
Race conditions and deadlocks: detection and prevention
handsOn: Multithreaded producer-consumer program using mutex and semaphores
Thread-safe counter with mutex synchronization
modules.learningOutcome: Write concurrent C programs using POSIX threads with proper synchronization mechanisms.

modules.title: Network Programming in C
topics: Socket programming concepts and the client-server model
BSD socket API: socket(), bind(), listen(), accept(), connect()
Sending and receiving data: send(), recv()
TCP vs UDP sockets
handsOn: Build a TCP echo server and client in C
modules.learningOutcome: Build networked client-server applications using the BSD socket API in C.

modules.title: Debugging & Performance Profiling
topics: Advanced GDB: watchpoints, core dump analysis, backtrace
Valgrind: memcheck (leak detection) and callgrind (profiling)
gprof for flat and call-graph profiling
AddressSanitizer (ASan) and UndefinedBehaviorSanitizer (UBSan)
modules.learningOutcome: Profile and optimize C programs using advanced debugging and performance measurement tools.

modules.title: Build Systems & Libraries
topics: Makefile structure: targets, prerequisites, and recipes
Variables, pattern rules, and automatic variables ($@, $<)
CMake basics: CMakeLists.txt, targets, properties
Creating and linking static libraries (.a files)
handsOn: Write a multi-target Makefile for a multi-file C project
Build and link a custom static math library
modules.learningOutcome: Manage complex C projects using Make and CMake build systems with static and shared libraries.
